message.reasoning_content:
We are given 10 trajectory evidence cards from round 2 of LunarLander-v2 task. All trajectories have:
- episode length = 1000 (max steps), truncated=true, terminated=false. So they all timed out.
- All have zero leg contact in last 20% steps (contact_ratio_last20 = 0.0). So no landing.
- All have high distance improvement (around 0.82 to 0.87) but final distance to target is around 0.54 to 0.60, meaning they are still at some distance from pad (not zero).
- Final speeds: vertical speed abs is small (0.003-0.025), horizontal speed abs varies (0.007 to 0.08). So they are hovering but not on pad.
- Final angle abs small (0.003 to 0.049 rad), angular velocity abs not given but likely small.
- No leg contact. So they never landed.

Labeling cautions: success_like requires both legs contact, near-zero velocities, small angle, within pad area. None satisfy that. They have no leg contact, so can't be success_like.
They show high progress (distance improvement) but no landing. They are not early failure because they lasted full episode. They are not low_progress_survival because they did make significant progress (improvement >0.8). But they are not partial_progress? They show real progress but not success. Since they hovered near pad but didn't land, and timed out, that matches partial_progress: trajectory shows real progress but does not reach stable success. Also they have good stability (low speeds, small angle) but no contact. So I think partial_progress is appropriate.

None are ambiguous because evidence is consistent: all truncated, no leg contact, good progress but no landing. So use_for_tage_pair = true for all.

Thus for each trajectory, label: partial_progress, use_for_tage_pair: true.
